name: compare_slice_reflexive
reason: Extensional equality should be reflexive — if it weren't, `compare_slice(x, x)` could return false

name: compare_slice_symmetric
code:
proof fn phi_2_compare_slice_symmetric(x: Seq<u8>, y: Seq<u8>)
    requires
        x =~= y,
    ensures
        y =~= x,
{
}
reason: Extensional equality should be symmetric — if comparison were order-dependent, it would violate basic equality semantics

name: compare_slice_empty_equal
reason: Two empty slices should always compare equal — if the loop or length check mishandled zero-length inputs, this could fail

name: compare_slice_different_lengths_not_equal
code:
proof fn phi_4_compare_slice_different_lengths_not_equal(x: Seq<u8>, y: Seq<u8>)
    requires
        x.len() != y.len(),
    ensures
        !(x =~= y),
{
}
reason: Sequences of different lengths should never be extensionally equal — if `=~=` ignored length, the early return would be unsound

name: compare_slice_single_diff_not_equal
code:
proof fn phi_5_compare_slice_single_diff_not_equal(x: Seq<u8>, y: Seq<u8>, k: int)
    requires
        x.len() == y.len(),
        0 <= k < x.len(),
        x[k] != y[k],
    ensures
        !(x =~= y),
{
}
reason: A single differing element should make the sequences unequal — if `=~=` were too coarse-grained, the element-wise comparison loop would be incorrect
